name: Stuffed Pork Cutlets
recipeIngredient: 8 boneless lean pork cutlets, 1/4 inch thick
1 cup Italian-style seasoned dried bread crumbs
1/4 cup light olive oil or vegetable oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim away any fat or connective tissues from pork.
2. Between 2 sheets of waxed paper, pound each cutlet with flat end of a meat mallet (or a heavy can) until flattened to about 1/8 inch thick.
3. Trim cheese and ham to a size slightly smaller than cutlets.
4. Top four of the cutlets with a slice of cheese and a slice of ham. Cover with remaining cutlets and pound edges lightly to seal together.
5. Dip each sandwich in egg, then into bread crumbs to coat completely. Place on waxed paper.
6. In a heavy, 10-inch skillet over medium-high heat, heat oil until rippling. Add cutlets and cook until browned on both sides, about 3-4 minutes per side.
7. Remove from pan with a spatula, draining off excess oil, and serve hot garnished with lemon wedges.
